# BrainStormer: Hooks

<!-- Generated by BrainStormer init — 2026-03-25 -->

## What Are Hooks?

Hooks are attention-grabbing openers — the first sentence, visual, or framing device that stops the scroll. Each hook can be paired with any angle to create content.

---

## Pattern Hooks

### The Paradox Hook
Template: "[Opposing Source A] and [Opposing Source B] just agreed on [specific fact]. Here's why that matters."
- "Fox News and The Guardian just confirmed the same number. Here's why that matters more than any opinion."
- "Al Jazeera and The Jerusalem Post published the same claim on the same day."
- "Breitbart and Mother Jones agree: [specific fact]. When was the last time that happened?"

### The Question Hook
Template: "What if [commonly held belief] was wrong — and you could prove it in 30 seconds?"
- "What if the most accurate journalist in America doesn't work for a newspaper?"
- "What if you could see, in real time, which facts no one disputes?"
- "What happens when you force four AIs to argue about the same headline?"

### The Stat Hook
Template: "[Number] sources. [Number] countries. [Number] bias tiers. One question: where do they converge?"
- "55 outlets. 7 regions. 7 bias tiers. Zero opinions. Just convergence."
- "We fed today's top story to 4 competing AIs. They agreed on 3 claims and fought about 2."
- "87% of today's top stories have at least one claim confirmed across the political spectrum."

### The Contrast Hook
Template: "Everyone's debating [opinion topic]. Meanwhile, here's what [all/most] sources actually agree on."
- "Twitter is arguing about whether the jobs report is good. Here's what every outlet — left, right, and center — agrees the number actually is."
- "The pundits are yelling about immigration policy. Here are the 4 facts that Fox, CNN, BBC, and Al Jazeera all confirmed."

### The Reveal Hook
Template: "We built a tool that [does surprising thing]. Here's what we found."
- "We built a tool that makes Fox News and MSNBC agree. Here's what happened."
- "We made four AIs read the same article independently. Two of them caught a claim the others missed."
- "We tracked 1,000 stories across 55 sources. The fringe outlets were right more often than you'd think."

### The Challenge Hook
Template: "You think you know what's true about [topic]? Check the convergence score."
- "You think you know what happened at the border this week? 47 outlets disagree with each other — except on these 3 facts."
- "Pick any headline from today. We'll show you which claims hold up across the spectrum."

### The Builder Hook
Template: "I [founder action] because [personal frustration]."
- "I built a news engine because I was tired of being lied to by people I'm supposed to trust."
- "I quit arguing on the internet and built a machine to settle it instead."
- "I spent 6 months feeding news to AIs. Here's what I learned about truth."

---

## Platform-Specific Hook Formats

### Twitter/X (< 280 chars)
- "Fox News and MSNBC agree on something. Triangulate found it. 🔺"
- "We don't show you bias. We show you where enemies agree."
- "4 AIs. 55 outlets. 7 continents. One question: what's actually true?"

### Reddit (title + first line)
- Title: "I built a tool that shows where ideologically opposed news outlets agree on the facts"
- First line: "Not another bias checker. This extracts specific claims and finds convergence across the political spectrum."

### LinkedIn (professional frame)
- "The future of information integrity isn't fact-checking. It's convergence. Here's why, and what I'm building."

### TikTok/Reels (spoken hook, < 3 sec)
- "Fox News and MSNBC just agreed on something and nobody's talking about it."
- "What if I told you four AIs argue about every headline before you see it?"
- "I built the thing that shows you where enemies agree."

### Substack / Newsletter
- "This week, 55 outlets covered the same 12 stories. They disagreed on almost everything — except these 5 facts."

### Product Hunt
- Tagline: "See where ideologically opposed news outlets confirm the same facts"
- First comment: "I built Triangulate because I was exhausted by news that's optimized for outrage instead of truth. It ingests 55+ outlets, clusters stories, extracts factual claims, and shows you the convergence — where enemies agree."
